Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets reason about the process: 1. The process starts with receiving the order (RO). 2. Then, we check availability (CA). 3. After availability is confirmed, we reserve items (RI). 4. Then, we pick the items (PI). 5. After picking, we do a quality check (QC). 6. Then, we pack the order (PO). 7. Then, we generate a shipping label (GSL). 8. Then, we dispatch the order (D). 9. At some point, we invoice the customer (IC) and then record the payment (RP).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We are not given the exact order of IC and RP relative to the other activities. Lets assume: IC occurs after D, and RP occurs after IC. Then the full order is: RO -> CA -> RI -> PI -> QC -> PO -> GSL -> D -> IC -> RP

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Equivalence: Typically, in such a process, some activities might have the same number of occurrences. Given the ambiguity, I think its safer to leave equivalence empty because we are not given explicit information that the occurrences are always the same. Therefore, we will leave the equivalence set empty.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before: This constraint says: if the first activity occurs, then the second activity should have been executed previously. For CA: if CA occurs, then RO must have occurred before. So (CA, RO) is an always_before pair. For RI: (RI, RO), (RI, CA) ... For RP: (RP, RO), (RP, CA), (RP, RI), (RP, PI), (RP, QC), (RP, PO), (RP, GSL), (RP, D), (RP, IC)

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After: This constraint is the opposite: if the first activity occurs, then the second activity is executed in one of the following events. For RO: (RO, CA), (RO, RI), ... For CA: (CA, RI), ... For IC: (IC, RP) For RP: nothing must occur after RP.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Never Together: This constraint says that two activities cannot co-exist in the same case. In a normal process, we expect all activities to occur in the same case. Therefore, we leave the never_together set empty.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences (activ_freq): We can set for each activity the allowed occurrence counts. Given the ambiguity, well set for every activity: {0,1}. This allows the activity to not occur or occur once.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Directly-Follows Constraints: In our sequence, we have immediate follow relationships: RO -> CA CA -> RI RI -> PI PI -> QC QC -> PO PO -> GSL GSL -> D D -> IC IC -> RP Well define: (RO, CA), (CA, RI), (RI, PI), (PI, QC), (QC, PO), (PO, GSL), (GSL, D), (D, IC), (IC, RP)

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now, lets construct the dictionary. Well represent: equivalence: set() always_before: set of pairs ... activ_freq: dictionary ... directly_follows: set of pairs
